tool_design.role_in_intervention: Primary daily execution record for the twice-daily temperature check.
tool_design.expected_output: Completed daily log with readings, action notes, and manager sign-off

failure_warnings.warning: Completed after the task instead of during it.
Treated as a tick-box without understanding thresholds.
failure_warnings.what_can_go_wrong: Boxes are ticked while risk remains.

content:
# Daily Temperature Log & Check Sheet **Tool ID:** T001-01 **Block:** B001 (Daily Temperature Monitoring) **Domain:** Food Safety & Hygiene (D01_food_safety) **Level:** L1 **Format:** Checklist **Role:** Opening Lead / Afternoon Shift Lead / Any trained food handler **Time Required:** 5-10 minutes per round **Version:** 2.0 --- ## What This Tool Is For Use this sheet to complete and prove the two temperature rounds that protect your cold chain every day. This is the evidence layer for `B001`. It should show, at a glance: - every fridge and freezer that matters - the reading taken at opening and mid-shift - who completed the check - what was done if a reading drifted high - whether the manager reviewed the day If this sheet is patchy, late, guessed, or filled in from memory, the whole food safety story weakens. --- ## Safe Ranges That Apply To This Sheet | Unit Type | Normal Working Range | Investigate At | Unsafe Trigger | |---|---|---|---| | Chilled fridge | `1-5C` | Above `5C` | `8C` or above | | Freezer | `-18C` or below | Above `-15C` | `-12C` or above | | Built-in digital display | Use as a guide only | Cross-check with probe | Probe result decides | **Rule:** If the reading is outside range, do not leave the row blank and move on. Record the reading, act, and note what happened. --- ## Pre-Check Setup
  • Probe thermometer is clean, sanitised, and switched on.
  • Probe calibration is current. If not, check [T001-02].
  • I know which product or water pot I am probing in each unit.
  • I have this sheet, a pen, and access to all cold units.
  • If a unit fails, I will log the action immediately instead of "fixing it later".
--- ## Printable Daily Log **Date:** ____________________ **Day:** ____________________ **Venue:** ____________________ | Unit Name | Product / Check Point | Safe Range | Opening Time | Opening Temp | Initials | Afternoon Time | Afternoon Temp | Initials | Action / Notes | |---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---| | Milk fridge | Middle milk carton / water pot | `1-5C` | | | | | | | | | Sandwich / prep fridge | Front top shelf product | `1-5C` | | | | | | | | | Grab-and-go display fridge | Warmest front-facing item | `1-5C` | | | | | | | | | Back-up fridge / walk-in | Middle shelf product | `1-5C` | | | | | | | | | Main freezer | Centre product / pack | `-18C` or below | | | | | | | | | Second freezer (if used) | Centre product / pack | `-18C` or below | | | | | | | | | Other cold unit | ____________________ | ____________________ | | | | | | | | --- ## If A Reading Is High ### Chilled units | Reading | What To Do Right Now | |---|---| | `5.1-7.9C` | Check door, airflow, stock loading, and whether the unit was recently opened. Recheck within 30 minutes and note action. | | `8C` or above | Inform the manager immediately, protect high-risk stock, and complete [T001-03]. Use [T003-02] if it becomes a true excursion event. | ### Frozen units | Reading | What To Do Right Now | |---|---| | `-14.9C` to `-12.1C` | Check door seal, frost build-up, and recent loading. Recheck and log action. | | `-12C` or warmer | Escalate immediately, keep door shut as much as possible, and complete [T001-03]. | --- ## End-Of-Day Review
  • Both temperature rounds were completed.
  • Any high readings were explained and logged.
  • Any linked corrective record in [T001-03] has been completed.
  • Manager reviewed the sheet before filing.
**Completed by:** ____________________ **Manager countersign:** ____________________ **Filed in SFBB / food safety folder:** ____________________ --- ## Common Mistakes To Avoid - Do not write a "normal" reading because the fridge usually runs fine. - Do not probe the air if your method is meant to check product temperature. - Do not skip the afternoon round because service got busy. - Do not leave "action taken" blank when a reading drifted high. - Do not use built-in displays as the only evidence when the SOP requires a probe reading. --- ## Connected Resources **Used with:** - T001-02 - Probe Calibration Record & Accuracy Tracker - T001-03 - Temperature Excursion Trigger Log - T001-04 - Monthly Temperature Compliance Dashboard - T001-05 - Temperature Monitoring SOP & Team Sign-Off **Related blocks:** - B002 - Cold Chain Management SOP - B003 - Temperature Excursion Response --- *Tool Version 2.0 - built to survive a busy shift and an unannounced inspection.*
